rulings.Case Title: CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS VS. SAEED KHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses And Responsive Documents Without Objection To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three, To Plaintiff Chi Chi Beignet, Inc. Dba Biscuits And Blues; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1565.00 Against Plaintiff And/Or Its Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2022 line 1. Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses And Responsive Documents Without Objection To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Three, To Plaintiff Chi Chi Beignet, Inc. Dba Biscuits And Blues; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1565.00 Against Plaintiff And/Or Its Counsel Of Record is GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL SERVE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, FULL AND CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN A MANNER CONSISTENT WITH THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATED ORDER REGARDING ESI AS WELL AS THE DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS. (2) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND ITS ATTORNEYS, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHOULD BE MADE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS VS. SAEED KHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant, Cross-Defendant And Cross-Complainant Saeed Khans' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Meiyan Enterprises, Inc.'S Further Responses To Document Demands And Production Of Electronically Created Documents In A Code Compliant Format
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2022 line 1. Defendant, Cross-Defendant And Cross-Complainant Saeed Khans' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Meiyan Enterprises, Inc.'S Further Responses To Document Demands And Production Of Electronically Created Documents In A Code Compliant Format is GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: MIEYAN'S OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION DOCUKENTS FROM DEFENDANT ARE OVERRULED. TO THE EXENT THAT IT HAS NOT PREVIOUSLY DONE SO, WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, MIEYAN WILL SERVE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, FULL AND CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO RFP NOS 5,6,8,9,11,12,14,22,23,25,26,27,32,34,36,39,40,41,42,44,45,47,48,49,50,51,52,53,56,62,63,64, 65,66 & 67 AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN A MANNER CONSISTENT WITH THE TERMS OF DEFENDANT'S DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION, INCLUDING ITS PROVISIONS FOR ESI MATERIALS. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT BY KAHN AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST EITHER PARTY OR ITS COUNSEL. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SHIMAMOTO VS. PAULA OGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT ITF 2, A PARTNERSHIP, STACEY SHIMAMOTO, STACEY SHIMAMOTO (INCORRECTLY SUED AS STACY, ITF, A PARTNERSHIP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. All causes of action subject to the demurrer are sufficiently stated. The propriety of expenditures by the defendants and the reasonableness of Plaintiff's reliance on representations are questions of fact not appropriate to be raised on a demurrer. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2022 line 14. DEFENDANT PAMELA RYAN, BRIAN COLE DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. First Amended Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Any issue regarding the expiration date of the permit served with the operative Notice is an issue of fact not appropriate for a determination on a demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KEVICH, TRUSTEE VS. IAN BROWN-GELB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Substituting Party And Leave Of Court To File Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 11, 2022 line 15. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY KEVICH, TRUSTEE Motion For Order Substituting Party And Leave Of Court To File Amended Complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
